hero:
# IPL Analytics

**Ball-by-ball T20 win-probability prediction · Python · Pandas · Scikit-learn**

## Hero Overview

A ball-by-ball win-probability predictor for T20 IPL matches: phase-level analysis (Powerplay, Middle, Death) and 25+ match-state features feed classification models that update win probability as the match unfolds.

An Elo-style rating layer tracks long-term team strength, giving live win-probability estimates a longer-horizon context.

points.evidence: Ran phase-level EDA on T20 IPL matches with Pandas, surfacing scoring-dynamic patterns aligned with public research.
Engineered 25+ ball-by-ball match-state features (overs, wickets, run-rate, required-rate) across team, player, venue and conditions.
Trained Random Forest, Logistic Regression and SVM to predict ball-by-ball win probability; Random Forest best at ~80%, surfaced as live win-probability updates.
Layered an Elo-style rating system tracking long-term team strength to contextualize match-state win-probability estimates.

network_graph.title: Technology Capability Network
network_graph.description: Maps the project's core techniques to the tools used to implement them.

storyline:
# Storyline

## Context

T20 match outcomes shift ball by ball with match state (overs, wickets, run-rate); flat match-level stats miss this in-game movement.

## Workflow

Phase-level EDA → ball-by-ball match-state feature engineering → win-probability classification → Elo-style team-rating layer.

## Technical Implementation

- **EDA:** phase-level analysis (Powerplay/Middle/Death) with Pandas, surfacing scoring patterns consistent with public T20 research.
- **Features:** 25+ ball-by-ball match-state features across team composition, player metrics, venue and conditions.
- **Win-probability modeling:** Random Forest, Logistic Regression and SVM predicting win probability; Random Forest best at ~80%.
- **Elo-style ratings:** a long-term team-strength layer contextualizing live win-probability estimates.

## Outcomes

Produced ball-by-ball win-probability estimates with an Elo-style long-term rating layer, framed for cricket-domain audiences.
